comment: "I found the section on assessing and managing trauma in pregnant women the most interesting. It clearly explained how physiological changes in pregnancy affect trauma response and guided me on how to prioritize care to improve both maternal and fetal outcomes."

"I enjoyed learning about trauma and physical injuries and how they can affect the baby and mother - but before it affects them what I can do to save both of them. I loved the way this courses is about saving the lives of two people."
"The feedback and explanations after the post-course assessment were an amazing added benefit."

"What I found most interesting was the unique overlap between maternal and fetal physiology in trauma management: while the pregnant mother is the first priority, properly treating her also leads to the best outcomes for the fetus."

"The aspects I found most interesting were the practical applications and case-based scenarios. I appreciated how the course linked theoretical concepts to real-life clinical situations, particularly in emergency and critical care settings. The interactive modules and step-by-step explanations of procedures helped me understand complex topics more effectively."
